{"id":"project-LICHEN-2h1i.5","title":"task","description":"## Why Yggdrasil
Global LICHEN mesh needs gateways to find each other. Options:

| Option | Address space | Setup |
|--------|--------------|-------|
| Tailscale | 100.x.y.z + fd7a | Easy, requires account |
| WireGuard | Manual config | Self-hosted, more work |
| Yggdrasil | 0200::/7 | Zero config, peer-to-peer |

## Yggdrasil fits LICHEN philosophy
- Addresses from Ed25519 pubkeys (LICHEN already uses these!)
- No central authority
- Self-healing routing
- Works over any transport

## Architecture
```
[Mesh A Portland]    [Mesh B Berlin]    [Mesh C Tokyo]
       |                   |                  |
   Gateway A           Gateway B          Gateway C
       |                   |                  |
       +------- Yggdrasil network -----------+
```

## Implementation
1. Gateway runs Yggdrasil peer
2. Mesh gets /64 from gateway Yggdrasil /128
3. Or: derive Yggdrasil addresses from same Ed25519 keys
4. Global routing just works

## Addressing
- Mesh-internal: ULA fd00:lichen::/32 + IID from Ed25519
- Global backbone: Yggdrasil 0200::/7
- Internet access: Tailscale/WG from gateway, or Yggdrasil exit nodes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-17T23:58:56.119586Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-17T23:58:56.119586Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2h1i.5","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2h1i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-17T23:58:56.244611Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
